rationale.R1.1: Artifact makes a compound contribution claim (adaptive termination + role-conditioned debate + image-encoder agnosticism); checking requires reconstructing whether each sub-claim is coherent and consistent with the described system.
rationale.R1.2: The core inferential chain — debate dynamic (not scale) drives gain, evidenced by ablation — must be internally consistent; logical gaps between mechanism description, ablation design, and conclusion are a primary check target.
rationale.R1.3: Specific F1 deltas are cited (e.g. 0.408 vs 0.391 on MIMIC-CXR) and used to justify the contribution; verifying that reported numbers actually support the stated conclusions is central to any 'check this' on a results-bearing paper.
rationale.R1.4: The entire framing positions RadCollab as a successor/improvement over RadCollab-base; assessing whether the delta is substantive and whether the novelty claim (adaptive convergence) is adequately distinguished from prior work is a plausible primary focus.
